identity.role: Golay -> Leech error-correction tower with exact construction receipts

pattern: complete-unformalized work -> formalization in paper-bound space -> Forge

source_work.state_before: VERIFICATION_REPORT.md records E8 roots 240, Weyl order 696729600, Leech kissing 196560 as asserted operational constants; no construction, no proof artifact, no forge

adjudicated_divergence: all three report constants are now computed, not cited: the Golay code is generated from a bordered QR(11) basis, the Steiner property verified by exact bijection, and all 196,560 minimal vectors constructed and membership-checked

construction detail surfaced during formalization: the bordered circulant requires the NON-residue convention — the residue convention produces a [24,12] code with weight-7 words (not Golay); the weight-enumerator check caught it on first run
validated_claims: The generator basis spans exactly 4096 distinct codewords ([24,12]).
The code is self-dual with minimum distance 8.
Steiner S(5,8,24): every 5-subset of 24 points lies in exactly one of the 759 octads (759*56 = 42,504 = C(24,5)).
The three minimal-vector shapes count 1,104 + 97,152 + 98,304 = 196,560 — all distinct, all norm^2 4, all lattice members under the exact membership predicate.
Sampled sums of minimal vectors remain lattice members with even norms.
No lattice vector of norm^2 = 2 exists (exhaustive case analysis over candidate shapes).
Tower consistency: 196,560 = 240 * 819, with E8Forge computing the 240 on the level below.
The CMPLX report constants (240, 696729600, 196560) are reproduced by computation.
honesty_boundary: Identification of the constructed lattice with THE Leech lattice rests on the uniqueness theorem (even unimodular, dim 24, min norm 4) — cited, not verified; closure is sampled, not exhaustive; 24D kissing optimality is cited mathematics.
open_obligations: Gram/unimodularity receipt for the constructed lattice basis.
Patch CMPLX VERIFICATION_REPORT upstream to reference the computed receipts instead of asserted constants.
Next profile-repo candidates: CMPLX-TMN-main kappa = ln(phi)/16 constant (already live in ChromaForge delta_phi receipts), CMPLXMCP AGRM controller, CMPLXDevKit MDHG cache laws.
notes: Seventh instance of the promotion pattern; second from the profile-repo lane. With E8Forge (paper 08) and LeechForge (paper 17), the error-correction tower of the corpus is now computationally receipted end to end: LCR triads -> Hamming/E8 -> Golay/Leech.
